headline: United Airlines Targets Distressed Rival Assets To Refine Premium Network Growth
summary: United Airlines Holdings CEO Scott Kirby signaled interest in buying airport slots, gates, and other key assets from financially stressed competitors, while ruling out large mergers. The comments highlight a focus on targeted asset deals during a period of higher fuel costs and potential pressure on weaker airlines. For investors tracking NasdaqGS:UAL, this potential shift in how United grows comes with the stock at $109.63 and a 1 year return of 31.8%. Over 3 years, the stock return is...
